mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, higher borrowing costs for corporations and increased discount rates for future cash flows reduce equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest FEDFUNDS value is 4.94, which is above the candidate threshold.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a tight labor market and robust economic activity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns. This is an always-on factor signal.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is 4.7%, historically indicating a relatively healthy labor market.
failure_condition: The signal's information coefficient (IC) is negative over any 3-year rolling window, indicating a negative correlation between low unemployment levels and forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) is coupled with a sharp deceleration in housing starts (HOUST), it signals a late-cycle environment where consumer strength is peaking while interest-rate sensitive sectors are faltering, leading to negative equity returns as the market anticipates a slowdown.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects broad economic strength and robust corporate activity, leading to higher earnings and positive equity market returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate regime, an acceleration in CPI inflation signals that monetary policy will remain restrictive for longer, increasing discount rates and compressing equity multiples, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 4.94%, indicating a high rate regime.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active during a tightening regime, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth below a critical threshold indicates a rapid cooling of the labor market, raising recession fears and causing a negative repricing of equities.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is measured in thousands, so a trigger of 100 corresponds to 100K jobs.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the average return on non-trigger days (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) provides a fundamental tailwind for corporate earnings across the economy, supporting higher equity prices over a medium-term horizon. This is a slow-moving, foundational signal.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 value is 11520.1, representing the broad economic backdrop.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.1 over any 10-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low and inflation is high, further strengthening in the labor market (i.e. a falling UNRATE) increases fears of wage-price spirals, forcing the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance, which is negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 4.7% meets the threshold condition.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is positive over a multi-year backtest.
